text: NdoŇč nna n…õ Ruben kaŇč…õ mo tuto le: ‚ÄúTa B…õnjamin m b…Ē…Ē ma en…Ē, meeŇč ya b…õta mo m ba. N…õ m maŇč ya b…õta mo m bar fo, fo m…Ē ma bibiny…õn any…Ē.‚ÄĚ

ref: But Jacob said, ‚ÄúI won't let my son Benjamin go down to Egypt with the rest of you. His brother is already dead, and he is the only son I have left. I am an old man, and if anything happens to him on the way, I'll die from sorrow, and all of you will be to blame.‚ÄĚ
text: Ama le n…õ Jeek…Ēb daŇč kaŇč…õ: ‚ÄúAyai, ma kebia ere maaŇč tu menyi n y…Ē ndoŇč. Mo da wu, mo nawule e baŇč ka na. N…õ menyee y…Ē n…õ t…Ēr…Ē ko ya tu mo enite na to, menyeeŇč shin n…õ kagbenejija e m…Ē ma ashi ma mbel to.‚ÄĚ

ref: until finally, Jacob's family had eaten all the grain they had bought in Egypt. So Jacob said to his sons, ‚ÄúGo back and buy some more grain.‚ÄĚ
text: SaŇč…õ na so n…õ Jeek…Ēb n…õ mbe basa mal…õ ji bumo be ajibi n…õ b daŇč ya t…Ē Ijipt na n loge n…õ mbia na bumo tuto Jeek…Ēb kaŇč…õ bumo: ‚ÄúMen naŇč b…õta n y…Ē Ijipt n naŇč ya t…Ē ajibi gbr…õbi m ba.‚ÄĚ

ref: Judah replied, ‚ÄúThe governor strictly warned us that we would not be allowed to see him unless we brought our youngest brother with us. If you let us take Benjamin along, we will go and buy grain. But we won't go without him!‚ÄĚ
text: Ama n…õ fo maaŇč shuli n wora loŇč bre, anyi maaŇč y…Ē Ňčkpal kany…õn na ka kaŇč…õ anyi fan…õ: ‚ÄėMen naŇč ba nfe Ňčkaa maŇč bar men sipo na, men sa maŇč shin n…õ n wu menyi so so.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ

ref: Jacob asked, ‚ÄúWhy did you cause me so much trouble by telling the governor you had another brother?‚ÄĚ
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ Jeek…Ēb bishi bumo: ‚ÄúMan…õ bre e ba n…õ men kaŇč…õ kany…õn nna fan…õ men kraa k…Ē esipo n ta le be t…Ēr…Ē ere a ba ma so?‚ÄĚ

ref: They answered, ‚ÄúHe asked a lot of questions about us and our family. He wanted to know if you were still alive and if we had any more brothers. All we could do was answer his questions. How could we know he would tell us to bring along our brother?‚ÄĚ
text: N…õ b kaŇč…õ mo: ‚ÄúKany…õn na gbagba e lege anyi to m bishi anyi, anyi gbagba n…õ anyi be kanaŇč to ebi be asheŇč. E y…õ: ‚ÄėMen tuto kraa w…Ēt…Ē a? Men kraa k…Ē esipo ny…õnso a?‚Äô Amoso mo ere be asheŇč bishiso na be afito n…õ anyi ere daŇč baa sa. Kumo be kaman nuso n…õ an daa wora a pin fan…õ e beeŇč kaŇč…õ fan…õ: ‚ÄėMen naa ba, men keta men sipo na a ba?‚Äô ‚ÄĚ

ref: Then Judah said to his father, ‚ÄúLet Benjamin go with me, and we will leave at once, so that none of us will starve to death.
text: Kamalga ere be kaman n…õ Juda kaŇč…õ mo tuto Jeek…Ēb: ‚ÄúTa kebia na m b…Ē…Ē na en…Ē n…õ an y…Ē epul ere to, saŇč…õ na so anyi n…õ fo n…õ anyi be mbia beeŇč baa w…Ēt…Ē Ňčkaa maaŇč wu.

ref: I promise to bring him back safely, and if I don't, you can blame me as long as I live.
text: Ma ere beeŇč keni mo so n…õ sh…õŇč maaŇč wora mo. N…õ kus…Ē kama wora mo n…õ m maŇč tiŇč m bar mo nfe m ba sa fo, meeŇč s…Ē kumo be turju ma Ňčkpa to kik…õ mbaanaay…Ē.

ref: If we had not wasted all this time, we could already have been there and back twice.‚ÄĚ
text: Keni, n…õ an daa maŇč jija saŇč…õ nna m ba fo mbre, n dafan…õ an y…Ē ndoŇč m b…õta m ba ale any…Ē gba.‚ÄĚ

ref: Their father said: If Benjamin must go with you, take the governor a gift of some of the best things from our own country, such as perfume, honey, spices, pistachio nuts, and almonds.
text: N…õ bumo tuto Jeek…Ēb kaŇč…õ bumo: ‚ÄúTo, n…õ k nya a du loŇč bre, men bela ase n ta kasawule ere so be as…Ērso lelaana ko n w…Ēt…Ē men be adiiku to n ya sa kany…õn na. Men ta tulale n…õ duwu n…õ as…Ē warabi n…õ mushoŇč gbr…õ ya sa mo.

ref: Also take along twice the amount of money for the grain, because there must have been some mistake when the money was put back in your sacks.
text: Kumo be kaman, men ta amansherbi ko n naŇč ti amo n…õ men daŇč wu men be eb…Ēt…õ to na so n y…Ē, Ňčkpal man…õ so, shere b wora nna n foe.

ref: Take Benjamin with you and leave at once.
text: Men ta men sipo gba n ti so n y…Ē naniere.

ref: When you go in to see the governor, I pray that God All-Powerful will be good to you and that the governor will let your other brother and Benjamin come back home with you. But if I must lose my children, I suppose I must.
text: Eny…õnpetale Eb…Ēr…õ na mal…õ e ya shin n…õ kany…õn na e wu menyi kuw…Ēr n shin n…õ men da n…õ e ka ndoŇč na n…õ B…õnjamin e tu menyi m ba. N…õ k ba fan…õ meeŇč paŇč ma mbia gba nna meeŇč paŇč bumo nna.‚ÄĚ

ref: he saw Benjamin and told the servant in charge of his house, ‚ÄúTake these men to my house. Slaughter an animal and cook it, so they can eat with me at noon.‚ÄĚ
text: Jos…õf ka wu B…õnjamin n…õ mo daana na n…õ e kaŇč…õ mbe laŇč to be ny…õrbi be enimu le: ‚ÄúKeta basa ere n y…Ē ma pe n ya m…Ē kus…Ēb…Ēya n wora ajibi n…õ ma n…õ bumo e ji kapa ere.‚ÄĚ

ref: The servant did as he was told and took the brothers to Joseph's house.
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ ny…õrbi be enimu na keta bumo n y…Ē Jos…õf pe fan…õ kanan…õ e kaŇč…õ mo na.

ref: But on the way they got worried and started thinking, ‚ÄúWe are being taken there because of the money that was put back in our sacks last time. He will arrest us, make us his slaves, and take our donkeys.‚ÄĚ
text: B ka fo Jos…õf pe na, kufu daŇč p…õ bumo nna, Ňčkpal man…õ so, b daa f…õ nna fan…õ amansherbi n…õ b daŇč b…õta n w…Ēt…Ē bumo be eb…Ēt…õ to saŇč…õ n…õ b fara m ba ndoŇč na so n…õ b bar bumo laŇč na to. B daa f…õ fan…õ e beeŇč p…õ bumo Ňč ki mbe anya nseŇč suge bumo be ekurma nna.

ref: So when they arrived at Joseph's house, they said to the servant in charge,
text: Ňäkpal le be nf…õra ere so, b ka fo Jos…õf be laŇč to n…õ b kaŇč…õ mbe ny…õrbi be enimu na le:

ref: ‚ÄúSir, we came to Egypt once before to buy grain.
text: ‚ÄúEbunyampo, an daŇč keba nfe nna m ba t…Ē ajibi.

ref: together with enough money to buy more grain. We don't know who put the money in our sacks.‚ÄĚ
text: Amo al…õ be buushi, an naŇč bar amansherbi ko n…õ an naŇč ba t…Ē ajibi. Ama hale mbre anyi maŇč nyi esa n…õ e ta amansherbi na n w…Ēt…Ē anyi be eb…Ēt…õ na to.‚ÄĚ

ref: The servant took them into Joseph's house and gave them water to wash their feet. He also tended their donkeys.
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ kay…õrbi nimuso na keta bumo n luri Jos…õf be kowu to n ya sa bumo nchu n…õ b f…Ēr bumo be aya so nseŇč sa bumo be ekurma ajibi.

ref: The brothers got their gifts ready to give to Joseph at noon, since they had heard they were going to eat there.
text: Kumo be kaman n…õ Jeek…Ēb be mbia na lara bumo be Ňčk…õ mo n…õ b bar Jos…õf na n yili a jo mo, Ňčkpal man…õ so, b daŇč nu fan…õ bumo n…õ mo e naa shin n…õ b ji kapaso be ajibi kam…Ēnch…õ na.

ref: When Joseph came home, they gave him the gifts they had brought, and they bowed down to him.
text: Jos…õf ka ba epe n…õ b j…ĒŇč…õ mo ase bunyanso nseŇč ta bumo be Ňčk…õ n…õ b bar mo na n sa mo.

ref: After Joseph had asked how they were, he said, ‚ÄúWhat about your elderly father? Is he still alive?‚ÄĚ
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ e ch…Ēr…Ē bumo nseŇč bishi bumo kanan…õ kebaaw…Ēt…Ē du n sa bumo n…õ kanan…õ bumo tuto du. E y…õ: ‚ÄúMen tuto kraa w…Ēt…Ē a?‚ÄĚ

ref: They answered, ‚ÄúYour servant our father is still alive and well.‚ÄĚ And again they bowed down to Joseph.
text: N…õ b naŇč j…ĒŇč…õ mo ase nseŇč shuli Ňč kaŇč…õ: ‚ÄúMm, an tuto fo kenya kraa w…Ēt…Ē a k…Ē alenfia.‚ÄĚ

ref: When Joseph looked around and saw his brother Benjamin, he said, ‚ÄúThis must be your youngest brother, the one you told me about. God bless you, my son.‚ÄĚ
text: E ka maŇč kumu so Ňč keni n wu mo sipo B…õnjamin n…õ e la mo nio pibi gbagba na n…õ e bishi bumo: ‚ÄúMen sipobi n…õ men kaŇč…õ ma mbe asheŇč na nde a?‚ÄĚ Kumo be kaman n…õ e kaŇč…õ: ‚ÄúM pibi, Eb…Ēr…õ e n…õfa fo.‚ÄĚ

ref: After washing his face and returning, he was able to control himself and said, ‚ÄúServe the meal!‚ÄĚ
text: E ka shu n loge nseŇč f…Ēr mbe anishito n…õ e lar a wora ania a p…õ mbe kumu nseŇč kaŇč…õ mbe ny…õrbi fan…õ b chige ajibi na to.

ref: Joseph was served at a table by himself, and his brothers were served at another. The Egyptians sat at yet another table, because Egyptians felt it was disgusting to eat with Hebrews.
text: B daŇč ta Jos…õf be ajibi nna n yili mo kakpa ko nseŇč ta mo daana na mal…õ n chena kakpa ko nseŇč ta Ijipt be basa n…õ b daa w…Ē ndoŇč na peya n yili kakpa ko. B daŇč wora loŇč nna, Ňčkpal man…õ so, k daa la ekishi nna fan…õ Ijipt be esa e tu Hibruw be esa n chena a ji.

ref: To the surprise of Joseph's brothers, they were seated in front of him according to their ages, from the oldest to the youngest.
text: B daŇč shin nna n…õ Jos…õf mo daana na chena n chena m b…õ abar so kenimu kenimuso loŇč n ya fo bumo be ekekarso. N…õ kumo be loŇč chinchiŇč bumo n…õ b keni Ňč keni abar.

ref: They were served food from Joseph's table, and Benjamin was given five times as much as each of the others. So Joseph's brothers ate and drank with him and had a good time.
text: Jos…õf be echenakpa na n…õ b daŇč yili n chige ajibi na n sa bumo. B…õnjamin mal…õ peya daa ch…Ē ekama peya ale anu. Ama bumo kik…õ daŇč ji kejigboŇč nna nseŇč tu Jos…õf n nuu as…Ēnuuso y…õly…õla so.

ref: Later, Joseph told the servant in charge of his house, ‚ÄúFill the men's grain sacks with as much as they can hold and put their money in the sacks. Also put my silver cup in the sack of the youngest brother.‚ÄĚ The servant did as he was told.
text: Kumo be kaman n…õ fo ta ma gbiti be kus…Ē nuunchu na n ti mbe ayu be amansherbi so n w…Ēt…Ē bumo be kaluge na be b…Ēt…õ to.‚ÄĚ NdoŇč nna n…õ kay…õrbi nimuso na wora as…Ē n…õ Jos…õf kaŇč…õ fan…õ e wora na kik…õ.

ref: Early the next morning, the men were sent on their way with their donkeys.
text: Kumo be Ňčklade, kaboŇč ka bee kpaŇč…õ to n…õ Jos…õf mo daana n…õ bumo be ekurma p…õ epe be ekpa.

ref: But they had not gone far from the city when Joseph told the servant, ‚ÄúGo after those men! When you catch them, say, ‚ÄėMy master has been good to you. So why have you stolen his silver cup?
text: B ka lar kade na to b daa maŇč naŇč fo kuf…Ē n…õ Jos…õf kaŇč…õ mbe kay…õrbi nimuso le: ‚ÄúJu m b…õ basa na so epul ere to. N…õ fo ya tu bumo fo bishi bumo le: ‚ÄėMan…õ e ba n…õ menyee ta kulubi a ka kelela be kuk…Ē?

ref: Not only does he drink from his cup, but he also uses it to learn about the future. You have done a terrible thing.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ
text: Man…õ e ba n…õ men yuri n ny…õnpe be gbiti be kus…Ēnuunchu? To, kumo n…õ e k…Ē a nuu as…Ē nseŇč naa k…Ē kumo a bishi so. Kus…Ē n…õ men wora ere maŇč wal…õ kuraa!‚Äô ‚ÄĚ

ref: When the servant caught up with them, he said exactly what Joseph had told him to say.
text: Kay…õrbi na ka ya tu bumo n…õ e ta asheŇč n…õ mo ny…õnpe kaŇč…õ na kik…õ chap Ňč kaŇč…õ bumo.

ref: But they replied, ‚ÄúSir, why do you say such things? We would never do anything like that!
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ b bishi mo le: ‚ÄúMan…õ e ba n…õ fo, an ny…õnpe bee malga le ere? Kaar…õ gba n…õ anyi, fo anya e wora loŇč!

ref: We even returned the money we found in our grain sacks when we got back to Canaan. So why would we want to steal any silver or gold from your master's house?
text: An ka daŇč ya wu amansherbi anyi be eb…Ēt…õ be nn…Ē to gba an daŇč shi Keenan m b…õta amo m ba. N…õ man…õ so n…õ anyee yuri fo ny…õnpe be gbiti Ňčko mbe shuwa ashi mo pe?

ref: If you find that one of us has the cup, then kill him, and the rest of us will become your slaves.‚ÄĚ
text: N…õ fo kaŇč keni n wu kumo anyi be ekama be b…Ēt…õ to, amodoŇčwura na beeŇč wu n…õ bumo n…õ b ka na mal…õ kik…õ e ki fo eny…õnpe na be anya.‚ÄĚ

ref: ‚ÄúGood!‚ÄĚ the man replied, ‚ÄúI'll do what you have said. But only the one who has the cup will become my slave. The rest of you can go free.‚ÄĚ
text: N…õ Jos…õf be kay…õrbi na kaŇč…õ bumo: ‚ÄúTo, n shuli, anyeeŇč wora kus…Ē n…õ men kaŇč…õ ere. Ekama n…õ k w…Ē mo kut…Ē beeŇč ki ma kenya n…õ bumo n…õ b ka na bre e baa w…Ē bumo be amu.‚ÄĚ

ref: Each of the brothers quickly put his sack on the ground and opened it.
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ bumo be ekama nya manaŇč n suge mbe b…Ēt…õ n yili m bugi kumo so.

ref: Joseph's servant started searching the sacks, beginning with the one that belonged to the oldest brother. When he came to Benjamin's sack, he found the cup.
text: N…õ Jos…õf be kay…õrbi na fara a keni bumo be as…Ē na to. Bumo kik…õ be enimuso peya to n…õ e fara Ňč keni keb…õso, keb…õso loŇč n ya fo bumo kik…õ to be kebia peya. E keni to loŇč n ya wu gbiti be kus…Ēnuunchu na B…õnjamin be b…Ēt…õ to.

ref: This upset the brothers so much that they began tearing their clothes in sorrow. Then they loaded their donkeys and returned to the city.
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ b kp…õa bumo be as…Ēbuusoana to Ňčkpal kagbenejijaso nseŇč naŇč b…õta Ňč kre bumo be esul…Ēana n deŇči bumo be ekurma so m b…õta n y…Ē kade na to.

ref: When Judah and his brothers got there, Joseph was still at home. So they bowed down to Joseph,
text: Jos…õf daa maŇč naŇč lar epe n…õ Juda n…õ mo sipoana na ba m ba dese kekil mbe anishito.

ref: ‚ÄúSir, what can we say?‚ÄĚ Judah replied. ‚ÄúHow can we say we are innocent, when God has shown we are guilty? And now all of us are your slaves, especially the one who had the cup.‚ÄĚ
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ Juda kaŇč…õ: ‚ÄúTo, naniere, man…õ n…õ an naa k…Ē n…õ an kaŇč…õ fo, an ny…õnpe? Man…õ n…õ anyeeŇč naŇč tiŇč Ňč kaŇč…õ Ňčko man…õ n…õ anyeeŇč naŇč tiŇč n wora Ňč Ňčini fan…õ anyi maŇč t…Ēr kap…ĒŇč? Eb…Ēr…õ shin n…õ fo anya be alubi di efuli, amoso naniere an ki fo anya, anyi n…õ emo n…õ b wu kus…Ēnuunchu na mbe kus…Ē to na kik…õ.‚ÄĚ

ref: Joseph told them, ‚ÄúI would never punish all of you. Only the one who was caught with the cup will become my slave. The rest of you are free to go home to your father.‚ÄĚ Judah Pleads for Benjamin
text: N…õ Jos…õf kaŇč…õ: ‚ÄúCha, kaar…õ gba n…õ n wora loŇč. Esa n…õ b wu ma kus…Ēnuunchu na nawule e naaŇč ki ma kenya. Menyi n…õ men ka na e b…õta a y…Ē epe men tuto kut…Ē n…õ kagbenewushi.‚ÄĚ

ref: Judah went over to Joseph and said: Sir, you have as much power as the king himself, and I am only your slave. Please don't get angry if I speak.
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ Juda y…Ē mo kut…Ē n ya kaŇč…õ mo: ‚ÄúN ny…õnpe jande, shin n…õ fo kenya e malga kamalgabi ko n sa fo, n ny…õnpe. N nyi fo n…õ ewura F…õ…õro ka sasa, ama jande, sa maŇč nya agbo n wora ma.

ref: You asked us if our father was still alive and if we had any more brothers.
text: Fo, n ny…õnpe e daŇč bishi anyi fo anya le: ‚ÄėMen k…Ē etuto Ňčko esipobi a?‚Äô

ref: So we told you, ‚ÄúOur father is a very old man. In fact, he was already old when Benjamin was born. Benjamin's brother is dead. Now Benjamin is the only one of the two brothers who is still alive, and our father loves him very much.‚ÄĚ
text: N…õ an shuli Ňč kaŇč…õ fo: ‚ÄėAn k…Ē etuto n…õ e bel a k…Ē kebiny…õnsobi n…õ e kurge mbe mbel to. Kebia na mo da wu n…õ e ki mo nawule ashi mo nio be mbia to n…õ mo tuto bee sha mo ga.‚Äô

ref: You ordered us to bring him here, so you could see him for yourself.
text: An ka kaŇč…õ fo loŇč na n…õ fo kaŇč…õ anyi, fo anya le: ‚ÄėMen ya bar mo n…õ ma al…õ gba e wu mo.‚Äô

ref: But you warned us that we could never see you again, unless our youngest brother came with us.
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ fo kaŇč…õ anyi, fo anya le: ‚ÄėN…õ men sipobi na baa maŇč tu menyi m ba, ma n…õ menyi maaŇč wu abar.‚Äô

ref: But we told him, ‚ÄúWe can't go back to Egypt without our youngest brother. We will never be let in to see the governor, unless he is with us.‚ÄĚ
text: N…õ an kaŇč…õ mo: ‚ÄėAyai, an sipobi ere baa maŇč tu anyi n y…Ē, anyi maaŇč tiŇč n y…Ē, Ňčkpal man…õ so, an maaŇč ya tiŇč n wu kany…õn na, ama an sipobi ere baa w…Ēt…Ē nna.‚Äô

ref: Sir, our father then reminded us that his favorite wife had given birth to two sons.
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ an tuto, fo kenya kaŇč…õ anyi: ‚ÄėMen nyi ma eche ka kurge bibiny…õnsobi any…Ē nawule.‚Äô

ref: One of them was already missing and had not been seen for a long time. My father thinks the boy was torn to pieces by some wild animal,
text: Eko maŇč naa w…Ē ma kut…Ē n…õ Ňč kaŇč…õ le: ‚ÄėKashenteŇčto kupuŇč to be kus…Ē ko e kp…õa mo to n we na.‚Äô Yili kumo be jeman…õ na so mal…õ kik…õ m ba fo mbre, m maŇč naŇč wu mo kik…õ.

ref: and he said, ‚ÄúI am an old man. If you take Benjamin from me, and something happens to him, I will die of a broken heart.‚ÄĚ
text: N…õ men naŇč ta ede mal…õ Ňč kut…Ē naniere n y…Ē n…õ kus…Ē ko naŇč ya nya mo al…õ, kagbenejija beeŇč m…Ē ma ashi ma mbel ere to.

ref: That's why Benjamin must be with us when I go back to my father. He loves him so much
text: Amoso n…õ m baŇč b…õta n y…Ē n tuto, fo kenya na kut…Ē Ňčkaa maŇč keta kebia na n y…Ē

ref: I promised my father that I would bring him safely home. If I don't, I told my father he could blame me the rest of my life.
text: Amoso p…Ē…õŇč n…õ an ba nfe ere, ma fo kenya nase k…Ēn…Ē nna fan…õ kebia na beeŇč naŇč b…õta n fo n tuto n…õ eyur be alenfia. Ňä kaŇč…õ n tuto nna fan…õ n…õ kebia na baa maŇč b…õta m ba epe, meeŇč s…Ē mbe turju ma Ňčkpa to mbaanaay…Ē!

ref: Sir, I am your slave. Please let me stay here in place of Benjamin and let him return home with his brothers.
text: Amoso, jande, shin n…õ ma fo kenya e s…Ē kebia na so n shir a w…Ē nfe Ňč ki fo kenya n…õ kebia na e tu mo daana ere m b…õta n y…Ē epe.

ref: How can I face my father if Benjamin isn't with me? I couldn't bear to see my father in such sorrow.
text: Ňäkpal man…õ so, n…õ kebia na maŇč tu ma n y…Ē epe, nuso n…õ meeŇč wora n y…Ē n tuto kut…Ē? Ayai, jande, sa maŇč shin n…õ n ya wu kagbenejija n…õ k beeŇč ba n tuto so na.‚ÄĚ

ref: Since Joseph could no longer control his feelings in front of his servants, he sent them out of the room. When he was alone with his brothers, he told them, ‚ÄúI am Joseph.‚ÄĚ
text: K ka fo le na, Jos…õf daa maŇč naŇč tiŇč n…õ e keta mbe kumu nseŇč kaŇč…õ basa p…Ēt…õ n…õ b daa w…Ē ndoŇč na le: ‚ÄúEkama e lar nfe Ňč ka anyi.‚ÄĚ Amoso esa p…Ēt…õ kama daa maŇč naa w…Ē Jos…õf kut…Ē saŇč…õ n…õ e lara mbe kumu Ňč Ňčini mo daana

ref: Then he cried so loudly that the Egyptians heard him and told about it in the king's palace.
text: nseŇč shu aw…Ērso ga hale n…õ Ijiptebi n…õ b daa w…Ē kowu na nu e ka bee shu. Ewura F…õ…õro be laŇč to ebi gba nu mbe kushu na be asheŇč.

ref: Joseph told them to come closer to him, and when they did, he said: Yes, I am your brother Joseph, the one you sold into Egypt.
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ Jos…õf kaŇč…õ mo daana na: ‚ÄúMen firgi a ba ma kut…Ē nfe.‚ÄĚ B ka firgi n taga mo to n…õ e kaŇč…õ bumo: ‚ÄúMa, men sipo Jos…õf n…õ men daŇč fa n sa Ijiptebi na nna na.

ref: There has already been a famine for two years, and for five more years no one will plow fields or harvest grain.
text: AkoŇč ere ka t…Ēr kumo be nf…õ any…Ē e kraa la na, kumo al…õ kraŇč ka nf…õ anu. Nf…õ anu na be jeman…õ so basa maaŇč tiŇč n d…Ē, a bishi n…õ b duu as…Ē Ňčko a teŇči.

ref: But God sent me on ahead of you to keep your families alive and to save you in this wonderful way.
text: Ama Eb…Ēr…õ shin nna n…õ n juŇčkpar menyi m ba nfe n…õ m ba b…Ēla le be ekpa ere so m m…Ēlga menyi, saŇč…õ na so, menyi n…õ men be kanaŇč to ebi maaŇč mur.

ref: After all, you weren't really the ones who sent me here‚ÄĒit was God. He made me the highest official in the king's court and placed me over all Egypt.
text: Amoso, mann…õ menyi e bar ma nfe, Eb…Ēr…õ e bar ma nfe m ba ta ma Ňč ki ewura F…õ…õro be kowu to ebi kik…õ nyam so be enimu n…õ mee ji kuwura Ijipt be efuli so kik…õ.

ref: Now hurry back and tell my father that his son Joseph says, ‚ÄúGod has made me ruler of Egypt. Come here as quickly as you can.
text: Amoso men nya manaŇč m b…õta n ya kaŇč…õ n tuto le: ‚ÄėFo pibi Jos…õf y…õ, Eb…Ēr…õ ta mo Ňč ki Ijipt be efuli so kik…õ be gomena, amoso nya manaŇč m ba kaa w…Ē mo kut…Ē nfe.

ref: You will live near me in the region of Goshen with your children and grandchildren, as well as with your sheep, goats, cattle, and everything else you own.
text: Fo ba feeŇč ya kaa w…Ē Goshen be kaba so. Fo n…õ fo mbia n…õ fo nanabiana n…õ fo as…Ēb…Ēya n…õ kus…Ē kama nyam n…õ fo k…Ē beeŇč ba kaa w…Ē nfe m mata ma.

ref: I will take care of you there during the next five years of famine. But if you don't come, you and your family and your animals will starve to death.‚ÄĚ
text: Fo ba kaa w…Ē Goshen, meeŇč baa keni fo n…õ fo kanaŇč to ebi so, Ňčkpal man…õ so, nf…õ anu mo n…õ a kraa w…Ē anyi be anishito ere kraa la akoŇč be nf…õ nna. Ma al…õ maa sha fan…õ akoŇč n…õ ketentaŇčase e m…Ē fo n…õ fo kowu to ebi n…õ fo basa kik…õ n…õ fo as…Ēb…Ēya kik…õ.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ

ref: All of you, including my brother Benjamin, can tell by what I have said that I really am Joseph.
text: Kub…Ēya na be kaman n…õ e kilgi Ňč kaŇč…õ mo daana na n…õ B…õnjamin: ‚ÄúMen gbagba wu nna na fan…õ ma Jos…õf gbagba e naa malga men kut…Ē na.

ref: Tell my father about my great power here in Egypt and about everything you have seen. Hurry and bring him here.
text: Men kaŇč…õ n tuto bunyaŇč n…õ b k…Ē a sa ma Ijipt nfe nseŇč kaŇč…õ mo kus…Ē kama nyam n…õ men wu nfe ere be asheŇč nseŇč bar n tuto nfe manaŇč.‚ÄĚ

ref: Joseph was still crying as he kissed each of his other brothers. After this, they started talking with Joseph.
text: Kumo be kaman n…õ Jos…õf n…õ mo daana na p…õ abar to kashaso n shu fraa. Ade be kaman n…õ mo daana na fara a malga mo kut…Ē y…õly…õla so.

ref: When it was told in the palace that Joseph's brothers had come, the king and his officials were happy.
text: Jos…õf mo daana na n…õ mo sipo B…õnjamin be keba na be baru ka fo ewura F…õ…õro pe n…õ mo n…õ mbe benimu kik…õ be Ňčgbene fuli bumo ga.

ref: So the king said to Joseph: Tell your brothers to load their donkeys and return to Canaan.
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ F…õ…õro kaŇč…õ Jos…õf le: ‚ÄúKeni, kaŇč…õ fo daana na fan…õ b bela ase n ta esul…Ē n sul…Ē bumo be ekurma m b…õta n y…Ē Keenan

ref: Tell them to bring their father and their families here. I will give them the best land in Egypt, and they can eat and enjoy everything that grows there.
text: n ya bar men tuto n…õ bumo be beche n…õ mbia nfe. N…õ men ba, meeŇč sa menyi Ijipt be kasawule lela ga n…õ men chena ndoŇč n nu ebel.

ref: Also tell your brothers to take some wagons from Egypt for their wives and children to ride in. And be sure they bring their father.
text: Kumo be kaman n…õ fo naŇč kaŇč…õ bumo le: ‚ÄėMen ta Ijipt nfe be egbaŇč…õturko n ti so n ya ta men be mbia n…õ men be beche n…õ men tuto n ti so m ba.

ref: They can leave their possessions behind, because they will be given the best of everything in Egypt.
text: Men sa maŇč ya shin n…õ men be as…Ē n…õ men maaŇč teŇč bar na be asheŇč e tir menyi, Ňčkpal man…õ so, menyeeŇč nya as…Ē lela Ijipt a ch…Ē amo.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ

ref: Jacob's sons agreed to do what the king had said. And Joseph gave them wagons and food for their trip home, just as the king had ordered.
text: NdoŇč nna n…õ Jeek…Ēb be mbia na wora asheŇč n…õ ewura F…õ…õro kaŇč…õ fan…õ b wora na kik…õ. N…õ Jos…õf b…õ F…õ…õro be k…Ēn…Ē so n sa bumo egbaŇč…õturko n…õ ekpa to be ajibi jiso.
